title: Matrices, Job Dependencies, Outputs, and Immutable Artifacts · Delivery-System Trace: Ci Matrix Axis Coverage
summary: A matrix silently excludes a supported platform, multiple jobs overwrite one artifact name, downstream work trusts a string output, and executable permissions disappear after archive transfer. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci matrix axis coverage increment in matrix and artifact handoff contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design include and exclude rules, experimental axes, fail-fast, max-parallel, names, and support-policy coverage without combinatorial waste.
Retain machine-readable reports, failed-case diagnostics, traces, screenshots, retries, quarantine owner, expiry, and re-entry criteria.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0

  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example matrix excludes one supported platform, overwrites artifact names, passes string outputs as booleans, and downloads an executable from another run. Prove graph results, types, producer identity, digest, layout, and trust. Competency probe: Design include and exclude rules, experimental axes, fail-fast, max-parallel, names, and support-policy coverage without combinatorial waste. A defensible solution applies Ci Matrix Axis Coverage, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to matrix and artifact handoff contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-matrix-axis-coverage — Design include and exclude rules, experimental axes, fail-fast, max-parallel, names, and support-policy coverage without combinatorial waste.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-gha-matrices-needs-outputs-artifacts-2
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-matrices-needs-outputs-artifacts-2
    artifact: handoff-contract.yaml
    decision: worked gha matrices needs outputs artifacts 2
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      matrix: node-22-24-by-ubuntu-macos-windows
      experimental: continue-only-declared-axis
      max-parallel: bounded
      needs: explicit-dag
      outputs: validated-json
      artifact-name: build-linux-x64-run8842
      artifact-digest: sha256-verified
      retention-days: 7
      hidden-files: excluded
    verify: enumerate matrix cases trace job results parse outputs and verify artifact ID digest layout retention and producer
    failure: Reject silent coverage exclusions, output type assumptions, artifact name reuse, hidden secret upload, or download as execution trust.
    repair: Prove matrix coverage, validate outputs, use unique immutable artifacts, verify digests and permissions, and bound retention.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Every matrix combination provides independent useful coverage. This fails the Ci Matrix Axis Coverage contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
